# Garden notes

Tomatoes want consistent watering; irregular soaking splits the fruit. The
raised bed on the south side dries out a full day earlier than the others.

Basil planted between the tomato rows seems to shade the soil enough to slow
evaporation, and the two crops get harvested together anyway.

Next season: try a drip line on a timer instead of hand watering, and keep a
log of which bed dries fastest.
